fields.description:
## Why
`origin/main` ALREADY has `rebar fsck --repair` (the A3 data-remediation verb: rebuild
snapshots, retire orphans; requires mutation; `--dry-run`/`--limit`). Rather than add a
colliding second mutation flag, fold the ensure-sweep into that existing "drive to healthy"
verb, and give plain read-only fsck an ensures report line. The server boot path should also
converge.

## What
FOLD `run_ensures()` (WS1) into the existing `fsck --repair` path (fsck_cli, fsck.py:~724
`# A3 remediation (--repair)`), so `--repair` converges BOTH data (existing) and config (new)
— NO new `--fix` flag. Run the ensure-sweep under the store write lock. Give plain read-only
`rebar fsck` (no `--repair`) an `ensures: N/M applied` line derived WITHOUT calling
run_ensures: M = count of registry ids (from `ensures.py`), N = count present in the parsed
`.ensure-applied` marker (WS1 format). Honor `no_mutate` (library read-only surfaces never run
the sweep). Wire MCP server startup (`mcp_server.main`, after the --help check) to call
`run_ensures()` best-effort — log-and-continue on failure, never abort `build_server().run()`;
under the write lock. Update the fsck help golden(s) if the report line changes help/usage, and
the fsck byte-parity tests (WS3 OWNS these; WS5 does not).

## Scope
In: folding run_ensures into `--repair`, the read-only `ensures: N/M` report line + its
marker+registry data-flow, no_mutate gating, MCP server-startup wiring (best-effort, locked),
fsck help-golden + byte-parity updates. Out: the registry/`run_ensures` (WS1), the write-path
hint (WS2), non-fsck behavioral tests (WS5).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rebar fsck --repair` additionally runs `run_ensures()` (folded into the existing A3 path) under the store write lock; NO new mutation flag is introduced
- [ ] `rebar fsck --repair` (ensure part) is git-level idempotent: two runs on a converged store produce zero new commits/pushes
- [ ] Plain read-only `rebar fsck` prints `ensures: N/M applied` (M=registry ids, N=in `.ensure-applied`) WITHOUT calling run_ensures; library `no_mutate=True` surfaces stay read-only and never sweep
- [ ] MCP server startup runs `run_ensures()` after the --help check, best-effort (log-and-continue, never aborts boot), under the write lock
- [ ] fsck byte-parity + help golden(s) updated and owned by WS3; a converged store's plain-fsck output changes only by the new report line
